body:
Closes #1992

Following the update to ClimaOcean v0.10 (#1971), `stop_time` would default to `Inf` in the ocean/sea-ice models, which forced us to use `Float`s instead of `Date`s. [This PR](https://github.com/NumericalEarth/NumericalEarth.jl/pull/395) in NumericalEarth.jl enables us to set `stop_time` as a kwarg. 

This PR:
- [x] Updates how `clock` and `stop_time` are set in the ocean model
- [x] Updates how `clock` and `stop_time` are set in the sea-ice model
- [x] Re-enables `use_itime: true` in config files

This PR also fixes a completely unrelated bug in `experiments/test/compare_cmip.jl` that emerged from an update in NumericalEarth (see [this PR](https://github.com/NumericalEarth/NumericalEarth.jl/pull/346); ocean surface salinity is now passed as a `view` instead of with `interior`, changing how we need to define `_compare(v1, v2)` for `SubArray`s).
title: Re-enable ITime in ocean and sea-ice models

body:
- ClimaCouplerClimaAtmosExt: query the radiation method via RRTMGP.radiation_method(solver) instead of reading the removed `.radiation_mode` field off the solver, which would throw on non-gray coupled runs (the neighboring flux accesses were migrated, this one missed).
- Rename rrtmgp_model -> rrtmgp_solver to match ClimaAtmos.
title: Update RRTMGP API (includes gates for CMIP env compat with older version)
Restore tracking SurfaceFluxes main in nightly pipeline
